legalAddress.text: 10903 New Hampshire Ave, Silver Spring, MD 20993, United States of America

name: A Phase I, Open Label, Dose Escalation Study of Oral Administration of INK128 in Combination with Paclitaxel, with/without Trastuzumab, in Subjects with Advanced Solid Malignancies

titles.text: A Phase I, Open Label, Dose Escalation Study of Oral Administration of INK128 in Combination with Paclitaxel, with/without Trastuzumab, in Subjects with Advanced Solid Malignancies

versions.rationale: To determine the maximum tolerated dose (MTD) and dose-limiting toxicity (DLT) of MLN0128 when administered in combination with paclitaxel in subjects with advanced solid malignancies.

population.name: Main Study Population
population.label: subjects with advanced solid malignancies

cohorts.name: HER2-
cohorts.label: HER2- cancer subjects
cohorts.description: Arm A will consist of approximately 12 to 15 safety-evaluable HER2- cancer subjects receiving MLN0128P at the MTD MLN0128P . Additional subjects may be enrolled into lower dose cohorts of MLN0128P of Arm A.

cohorts.name: HER2+
cohorts.label: HER2+ cancer subjects
cohorts.description: Arm B will consist of approximately 12 to 15 safety-evaluable HER2+ cancer subjects receiving MLN0128P at the MTD MLN0128P plus weekly trastuzumab (MLN0128PH). Up to 3 subjects will be enrolled initially in Arm B and observed for one cycle prior to enrolling additional subjects into this arm. Additional subjects may be enrolled into lower dose cohorts of MLN0128P + trastuzumab of Arm B.

population.description: This is a phase 1, open-label study consisting of a dose-escalation phase in advanced solid malignancies to determine the MTD of oral administration of MLN0128 (QDx3dQW on a 4-week cycle) or via alternate dosing schedules (such as 5 days on, 2 days off repeated weekly [QDx5d QW]) in combination with an infusion of paclitaxel on Days 1, 8, and 15 of each cycle (MLN0128P), followed by an expansion phase at the MLN0128P MTD (MTD MLN0128P ) ± trastuzumab in up to 25 additional subjects for further safety and preliminary efficacy.

arms.name: Arm A
arms.label: Arm A
arms.description: Arm A will consist of HER2- cancer subjects receiving MLN0128P at the MTD MLN0128P

arms.name: Arm B
arms.label: Arm B
arms.description: Arm B will consist of HER2+ cancer subjects receiving MLN0128P at the MTD MLN0128P plus weekly trastuzumab (MLN0128PH)

studyDesigns.name: MLN0128 Dose Escalation and Expansion Study Design
studyDesigns.label: Phase 1, open-label study

epochs.name: dose-escalation phase
epochs.label: Dose Escalation Phase
epochs.description: The dose-escalation phase of the study is to determine the MTD and the DLT of MLN0128 when given orally on Days 1-3 each week of a 4-week cycle (QDx3d QW) or via alternate dosing schedules (such as QDx5d QW) in combination with paclitaxel on Days 1, 8, and 15 of each cycle (MLN0128P) in subjects with advanced solid malignancies. A cycle is defined as 4 weeks.

epochs.name: expansion phase
epochs.label: Expansion Phase
epochs.description: After the MTD MLN0128P has been identified, 2 arms of subjects will be enrolled in parallel. Approximately 30 subjects may be enrolled in the expansion phase of the study: -  Arm A will consist of approximately 12 to 15 safety-evaluable HER2- cancer subjects receiving MLN0128P at the MTD MLN0128P . Additional subjects may be enrolled into lower dose cohorts of MLN0128P of Arm A. -  Arm B will consist of approximately 12 to 15 safety-evaluable HER2+ cancer subjects receiving MLN0128P at the MTD MLN0128P plus weekly trastuzumab (MLN0128PH). Up to 3 subjects will be enrolled initially in Arm B and observed for one cycle prior to enrolling additional subjects into this arm. Additional subjects may be enrolled into lower dose cohorts of MLN0128P + trastuzumab of Arm B.

elements.name: EL1
elements.label: infusion of paclitaxel on Days 1, 8, and 15 of each cycle
elements.description: in combination with an infusion of paclitaxel on Days 1, 8, and 15 of each cycle (MLN0128P)

transitionEndRule.name: End of Paclitaxel Infusion
transitionEndRule.text: on Days 1, 8, and 15 of each cycle

transitionStartRule.name: Start of Paclitaxel Infusion
transitionStartRule.text: on Days 1, 8, and 15 of each cycle

elements.name: EL2
elements.label: oral administration of MLN0128 (QDx3dQW on a 4-week cycle) or via alternate dosing schedules (such as 5 days on, 2 days off repeated weekly [QDx5d QW])
elements.description: oral administration of MLN0128 (QDx3dQW on a 4-week cycle) or via alternate dosing schedules (such as 5 days on, 2 days off repeated weekly [QDx5d QW])

transitionEndRule.name: End of MLN0128 Administration
transitionEndRule.text: on Days 1-3 each week of a 4-week cycle (QDx3d QW) or via alternate dosing schedules (such as QDx5d QW)

transitionStartRule.name: Start of MLN0128 Administration
transitionStartRule.text: on Days 1-3 each week of a 4-week cycle (QDx3d QW) or via alternate dosing schedules (such as QDx5d QW)

elements.name: EL3
elements.label: Dose Limiting Toxicity assessment
elements.description: Dose-limiting toxicity is defined as either of the following occurring during Cycle 1 (Days 1 - 28) and attributable to MLN0128P

estimands.name: EST_PR_01
estimands.label: Primary Estimand
estimands.description: The primary estimand for the expansion phase evaluates the objective response rate (ORR) in subjects with advanced solid malignancies receiving MLN0128 in combination with paclitaxel, with or without trastuzumab, using the treatment policy strategy for intercurrent events.
estimands.populationSummary: The population consists of all enrolled subjects in the expansion phase who receive at least one dose of study drug and have measurable disease at baseline, grouped by tumor histology cohorts (HER2- and HER2+).

intercurrentEvents.name: Disease Progression or Death
intercurrentEvents.text: Disease progression or death will be handled using the treatment policy strategy, where data after the intercurrent event are included in the analysis as observed.
intercurrentEvents.label: Intercurrent Event 1
intercurrentEvents.description: Disease progression or death due to any cause occurring after randomization but before the endpoint is measured.

intercurrentEvents.name: Initiation of New Anticancer Therapy
intercurrentEvents.text: Initiation of new anticancer therapy will be handled using the treatment policy strategy, including any subsequent observations.
intercurrentEvents.label: Intercurrent Event 2
intercurrentEvents.description: Start of a new anticancer treatment before the endpoint assessment.

studyDesigns.rationale: The proposed study design is standard for Phase I human oncology trials assessing safety and tolerability. Neither a placebo nor an active control will be included in this study.

activities.name: Vital signs, Height, Weight and ECOG PS
activities.label: Vital signs, Height, Weight and ECOG PS

activities.name: Radiographic Tumor Evaluation and Tumor Markers
activities.label: Radiographic Tumor Evaluation and Tumor Markers

activities.name: Trastuzumab Administration – Only in Arm B of Expansion Phase
activities.label: Trastuzumab Administration – Only in Arm B of Expansion Phase

objectives.name: OBJ_PR_01
objectives.text: To determine the maximum tolerated dose (MTD) and dose limiting toxicity (DLT) of MLN0128 when administered on a q3d qw schedule or via alternate dosing schedules such as q5d qw in combination with paclitaxel on Days 1, 8, and 15 of each cycle in subjects with advanced solid malignancies.
objectives.label: Primary Objective 1

endpoints.name: EP_PR_01
endpoints.text: Incidence of dose-limiting toxicities (DLT) during the first cycle of treatment.
endpoints.label: Primary Endpoint 1
endpoints.description: The maximum tolerated dose of MLN0128 will be determined when administered orally QDx3d QW and/or via alternate dosing schedules (such as QDx5d QW) in combination with weekly paclitaxel in subjects with advanced solid malignancies. The MTD of each dosing schedule is defined as the highest dose level of MLN0128 administered at which no more than 1 out of 6 evaluable subjects experiencing DLT during the first treatment cycle. The rate of DLT will be determined from the occurrence of adverse events which meet the criteria in Section 7.2.1.

objectives.description: To determine the maximum tolerated dose (MTD) and dose limiting toxicity (DLT) of MLN0128 when administered on a q3d qw schedule or via alternate dosing schedules such as q5d qw in combination with paclitaxel on Days 1, 8, and 15 of each cycle in subjects with advanced solid malignancies.

objectives.name: OBJ_PR_02
objectives.text: To evaluate the safety and tolerability of oral administration of MLN0128 q3d qw or via alternate dosing schedules such as q5d qw in combination with paclitaxel on Days 1, 8, and 15 of each cycle in subjects with advanced solid malignancies.
objectives.label: Primary Objective 2

endpoints.name: EP_PR_02
endpoints.text: Incidence, nature, and severity of adverse events (AEs).
endpoints.label: Primary Endpoint 2
endpoints.description: Safety and tolerability of MLN0128P and MLN0128PH will be assessed in each study phase (if applicable) based on the incidence, duration, and severity of treatment-emergent adverse events, including dose-limiting toxicity, serious adverse events, adverse events resulting in permanent discontinuation of protocol treatment, and deaths within 30-days of the last dose of protocol treatment; changes in laboratory test results including chemistry and hematology; changes in vital signs including blood pressure, pulse, and temperature; changes in electrocardiogram results.

objectives.description: To evaluate the safety and tolerability of oral administration of MLN0128 q3d qw or via alternate dosing schedules such as q5d qw in combination with paclitaxel on Days 1, 8, and 15 of each cycle in subjects with advanced solid malignancies.

objectives.name: OBJ_SEC_01
objectives.text: To evaluate the plasma PK of MLN0128 in combination with paclitaxel.
objectives.label: Secondary Objective 1

endpoints.name: EP_SEC_01
endpoints.text: Pharmacokinetic parameters of MLN0128 (Cmax, Cmin, Tmax, t½, AUC).
endpoints.label: Secondary Endpoint 1
endpoints.description: The plasma pharmacokinetics of MLN0128 in combination with paclitaxel will be determined from selected subjects based on the PK parameters listed below: Maximum plasma concentration (Cmax), Minimum plasma concentration (Cmin), Time of maximum plasma concentration (Tmax), Plasma half-life (t½), Area under the plasma concentration-time curve from time zero to infinity (AUC0-∞), zero to last dose (AUC0-last), and %AUC extrapolated.

objectives.description: To evaluate the plasma PK of MLN0128 in combination with paclitaxel.

objectives.name: OBJ_SEC_04
objectives.text: To evaluate the safety and preliminary anti-tumor activity of the combination of MLN0128, paclitaxel, and trastuzumab in subjects with HER2+ cancers.
objectives.label: Secondary Objective 4

endpoints.name: EP_SEC_04
endpoints.text: Objective response rate (ORR) and safety in HER2+ subjects receiving MLN0128 + paclitaxel + trastuzumab.
endpoints.label: Secondary Endpoint 4
endpoints.description: Arm B will consist of HER2+ cancer subjects receiving MLN0128P plus weekly trastuzumab (MLN0128PH). The objective response rate will be based on the subject's best overall tumor response documented during the course of protocol therapy. Objective response includes CR and PR and will be determined based on RECIST (version 1.1). Safety and tolerability assessed as in primary endpoints.

objectives.description: To evaluate the safety and preliminary anti-tumor activity of the combination of MLN0128, paclitaxel, and trastuzumab in subjects with HER2+ cancers.

objectives.name: OBJ_SEC_02
objectives.text: To evaluate the plasma PK of paclitaxel in combination with MLN0128.
objectives.label: Secondary Objective 2

endpoints.name: EP_SEC_02
endpoints.text: Pharmacokinetic parameters of paclitaxel (Cmax, Cmin, Tmax, t½, AUC, CL, Vss).
endpoints.label: Secondary Endpoint 2
endpoints.description: The plasma pharmacokinetics of paclitaxel in combination with MLN0128 will be evaluated in a similar manner. The additional intravenous PK parameters, CL, and Vss will be estimated for paclitaxel.

objectives.description: To evaluate the plasma PK of paclitaxel in combination with MLN0128.

objectives.name: OBJ_SEC_03
objectives.text: To evaluate the preliminary anti-tumor activity of oral administration of MLN0128 in combination with paclitaxel.
objectives.label: Secondary Objective 3

endpoints.name: EP_SEC_03
endpoints.text: Objective response rate (ORR), duration of response, and time to progression per RECIST v1.1.
endpoints.label: Secondary Endpoint 3
endpoints.description: The objective response rate will be estimated for each tumor histology cohort studied in the expansion phase. Arm A will consist of HER2- cancer subjects receiving MLN0128P; The objective response rate will be based on the subject's best overall tumor response documented during the course of protocol therapy. Objective response includes CR and PR and will be determined based on RECIST (version 1.1). The duration of objective response will be evaluated for subjects who achieve CR or PR. Radiographic and/or physical assessments of the malignancies will be made at Screening/Baseline (within 28 days before the first study drug administration) and every 6 weeks thereafter. Objective response (complete response [CR] and partial response [PR]) as determined by the subject's best tumor response duration response and time to progression will be assessed for all subjects using RECIST criteria version 1.1. A confirmatory CT/MRI scan should be performed approximately 3 weeks from the previous scan for all patients with an objective response and ≤ PR.

objectives.description: To evaluate the preliminary anti-tumor activity of oral administration of MLN0128 in combination with paclitaxel.

studyDesigns.description: This is a phase 1, open-label study consisting of a dose-escalation phase in advanced solid malignancies to determine the MTD of oral administration of MLN0128 (QDx3dQW on a 4-week cycle) or via alternate dosing schedules (such as 5 days on, 2 days off repeated weekly [QDx5d QW]) in combination with an infusion of paclitaxel on Days 1, 8, and 15 of each cycle (MLN0128P), followed by an expansion phase at the MLN0128P MTD (MTD MLN0128P ) ± trastuzumab in up to 25 additional subjects for further safety and preliminary efficacy. A cycle will be defined as 4 weeks.

scheduleTimelines.name: Main Timeline
scheduleTimelines.label: Main Timeline
scheduleTimelines.description: Main timeline for the study design.

footnotes.text: Height is only needed at Screening/Baseline.

Complete physical examination will be performed at Screening/Baseline, C1D1 and at the Termination visit. Symptom directed physical examination will be done on C1D8 (±1 day), C1D15 (±1 day) and C1D22 (±1 day).

All scheduled ECGs should be performed after the patient has rested quietly for at least 5 minutes in a supine position. When the timing of ECG assessments coincides with a blood collection, the ECG should be obtained prior to the nominal time of the blood collection. In some cases, it may be appropriate to repeat an abnormal ECG to rule out improper lead placement as contributing to the ECG abnormality.

Complete blood count (CBC) includes white blood cell count (WBC) with differential, hemoglobin (Hgb), hematocrit (Hct), and platelet count. CBC results performed pre-dose on Days 1, 8, 15 and 22 of Cycle 1 must be available and reviewed by the investigator prior to trastuzumab, paclitaxel, or MLN0128 dosing.

Full chemistry panel includes Chem 7 (sodium [Na], potassium [K] chloride [Cl], bicarbonate [CO₂], blood urea nitrogen [BUN], creatinine [Cr], glucose), liver function tests (LFTs - ALT, AST, alkaline phosphatase, total bilirubin, direct bilirubin), lactate dehydrogenase (LDH), total protein, calcium, phosphate, and magnesium. Chem 7 and LFT results performed pre-dose on Days 1, 8, 15, and 22 of Cycle 1 must be available and reviewed by the investigator prior to trastuzumab, paclitaxel, or MLN0128 dosing. Electrolyte levels should be corrected as needed before MLN0128 dosing, or before paclitaxel or trastuzumab dosing on days when paclitaxel or trastuzumab are administered 24 hours before MLN0128 dosing.

Fasting serum glucose will be measured prior to dexamethasone premedication for subjects receiving MLN0128P or prior to trastuzumab infusion for subjects receiving MLN0128PH on Cycle 1 Day 1 (C1D1), pre-MLN0128 dose on C1D22 when paclitaxel is not administered) for subjects receiving MLN0128PH on C1D8 (±1 day), C1D15 (±1 day), C1D22 (±1 day) and at the Termination visit. Additionally, upon implementation of Amendment 1 and under Amendment 2, fasting blood glucose will be measured at 1 hour post-MLN0128 dosing on C1D2 when PK samples are drawn in the dose escalation phase only.
Total cholesterol, high density lipoprotein cholesterol (HDL-C), low density lipoprotein cholesterol (LDL-C), and triglycerides.

Urinalysis will include macroscopic assessment of the amount of protein, glucose, white blood cells and blood if they are present (levels should be recorded if available) and microscopic analysis should be performed if an abnormality is noted. Microscopic urinalysis will also be performed along with a 12-hour urine collection and spot urine electrolytes, protein and creatinine, and serum chemistry at any time when the serum creatinine is ≥ Grade 1 according to NCI CTCAE version 4.0.
Only for female subjects with child-bearing potential. Serum pregnancy test will be done at Screening/Baseline. Either serum or urine pregnancy test will be done on C1D1.

Plasma PK samples will be obtained for subjects in the dose escalation phase prior to determination of the MLN0128P MTD at the following time points under the original protocol: pre-dose, immediately at the end of paclitaxel infusion (EOPI and pre-MLN0128 dose), and at 0.5 hour (±15 min), 1 hour (±15 min), 2 hours (±30 min), 4 hours (±30 min), 6 hours (± 30 min), and 24 ±2 hours post-MLN0128 dose on C1D1. The PK sample drawn on C1D22 (24 hours post-MLN0128 dosing) will be obtained before C1D2 MLN0128 dosing. Upon implementation of Amendment 1, plasma PK samples will be obtained for subjects in the dose escalation phase of the study at the following time points: on C1D1, pre-dose, immediately at EOPI, and at 0.5 hour (±15 min), 1 hour (±15 min), 2 hours (±30 min), 4 hours (±30 min), and 6 hours (±30 min) post-paclitaxel infusion completion; and on C1D2, pre-MLN0128 dose, and at 0.5 hour (±15 min), 1 hour (±15 min), 2 hours (±30 min), 4 hours (±30 min), and 6 hours (±30 min) post-MLN0128 C1D2 dose.

Fresh tumor tissue biopsies will be taken from subjects who have accessible tumor tissues based on the Investigator’s judgment and who have signed the consent for tumor biopsy. Fresh tumor tissue collections should coincide with the collection of PK samples at 3 hours ± 1 hour post-MLN0128 dose.

Every effort should be made for all subjects who have available archival tumor tissues to collect either paraffin blocks or a minimum of 10 unstained slides of archival tumor tissue for assessment of predictive/prognostic markers. Archival tumour tissues can be collected after the subject begins study drug treatment, if extra time is needed to locate the specimens.

Baseline CT (with contrast)/MRI of chest, abdomen and pelvis and relevant tumor markers (eg, cancer antigen [CA]125, prostate-specific antigen [PSA], CA19-9, CA27.29, CA15.3, and carcinoembryonic antigen [CEA]) must be obtained within 4 weeks prior to the first dose. A confirmatory scan should be performed at approximately 4 weeks from the previous scan for all subjects with an objective response of ≥ PR. The same method (CT with contrast or MRI) must be used throughout the study.

Subjects will be given a glucometer on C1D2 to monitor daily fasting whole blood glucose level at home and will be instructed to notify the study doctor when the fasting glucose is abnormal. The early detection and treatment of hyperglycemia has been key in managing this drug-related adverse event effectively in previous human studies with MLN0128. Each institution should decide an abnormal fasting glucose level (ie, ≥ 140 mg/dL) where the treatment and close monitoring of hyperglycemia might be required.
Adverse events will be collected after administration of the first dose of MLN0128, paclitaxel or trastuzumab through 30 days following the last dose of study drug.
Concomitant medications will be collected from 30 days prior to the first dose of study drug (MLN0128, paclitaxel or trastuzumab) until termination of the study.

Prior to Amendment 1, MLN0128 was initially to be given orally on a schedule consisting of daily administration of MLN0128 for 3 days on, 4 days off repeated each week (QDx3d QW of a 4-week cycle). On days when weekly paclitaxel is administered (Days 1, 8 [±1 day], and 15 [±1 day] of every 28-day cycle), MLN0128 was to be administered in the clinic to the subject within 30 minutes of the completion of paclitaxel infusion. On Day 22 (±1 day) when paclitaxel is not administered, MLN0128 was to be administered alone for subjects receiving MLN0128P and within 30 minutes of the completion of trastuzumab for subjects receiving MLN0128PH. The starting dose of MLN0128 is 6 mg daily for the first 3 days (Days 1-3) of each week. Upon implementation of Amendment 1 in subsequent dose escalation cohorts, for the intermittent dosing schedule evaluated, MLN0128 is administered at approximately 24 hours following paclitaxel infusion during Weeks 1-3. For example, in the QDx3d QW dosing schedule, MLN0128 will be administered on Days 2-4, 9-11, 16-18, and 22-24 of each 4-week cycle; and, in the QDx5d QW dosing schedule, MLN0128 will be administered on Days 2-6, 9-13, 16-20, and 22-26 of each 4-week cycle. Electrolyte levels should be corrected as needed prior to MLN0128 dosing on days when paclitaxel is not administered. On days that paclitaxel is administered (with or without trastuzumab), electrolyte levels should be corrected prior to paclitaxel or trastuzumab dosing.

Paclitaxel 80 mg/m² will be given intravenously weekly on Days 1, 8 (±1 day), and 15 (±1 day) of every 28-day cycle according to the FDA label and institution’s standard clinical practice. Concomitant medications used prior to and during the administration of paclitaxel will be recorded on the appropriate CRF pages. Prior to Amendment 1, on days when paclitaxel was administered, MLN0128 was to be given orally in the clinic within 30 minutes of completion of the paclitaxel infusion; and upon implementation of Amendment 1, MLN0128 will be given orally at approximately 24 hours following paclitaxel infusion. If paclitaxel dosing is altered ±1 day, MLN0128 administration should also be altered accordingly. Electrolyte levels should be corrected as needed prior to paclitaxel dosing (see footnote 19 for days when trastuzumab is administered).

Once the MTD of MLN0128P (MTDMLN0128P) is determined for the dosing schedules evaluated, Arm B of the expansion phase will enroll HER2+ cancer subjects. These subjects will receive weekly trastuzumab in combination with MLN0128P at the MTDMLN0128P on Days 1, 8 (±1 day), 15 (±1 day) and 22 (±1 day) of every 28-day cycle. Trastuzumab 4 mg/kg loading dose will be given on C1D1, with subsequent weekly doses at 2 mg/kg thereafter. For subjects entering expansion Arm B already receiving trastuzumab, the C1D1 dose will be 2 mg/kg if the last trastuzumab dose had been administered within 28 days. If the last trastuzumab dose was administered >28 days before C1D1, then the 4 mg/kg loading dose will be administered. Trastuzumab will be administered intravenously prior to paclitaxel and according to the FDA label and institution’s standard clinical practice. On Day 22 (±1 day) when paclitaxel is not administered, MLN0128 will be administered within 30 minutes of the completion of the trastuzumab infusion. Concomitant medications used prior to and during the administration of trastuzumab will be recorded on the appropriate CRF pages. Electrolyte levels should be corrected as needed prior to trastuzumab dosing when trastuzumab is administered before paclitaxel.
Termination visit will be done within 30 days of last dose of MLN0128 or paclitaxel or trastuzumab, whichever is discontinued last.
Upon implementation of Amendment 2 subjects in the expansion phase were not required to return to clinic on C1D2.
The assessment does not need to be repeated if it was done within 3 days prior to the first dose.

12 lead ECG 4 hours (± 30 min) after MLN0128 dosing on Day 1 for subjects enrolled on the original protocol and 4 hours (± 30 min) post-dose on Day 2 for subjects enrolled following implementation of Amendment 1. Upon implementation of Amendment 2, ECG is only performed for subjects in the dose escalation phase on C1D2.
Tumor assessments will only be done on subjects who have not previously demonstrated disease progression in the study unless completed within the previous 4 weeks.
Height is only needed at Screening/Baseline.

Complete physical examination will be performed at Screening/Baseline, C1D1 and at the Termination visit. Symptom directed physical examination will be done on C1D8 (±1 day), C1D15 (±1 day) and C1D22 (±1 day).

All scheduled ECGs should be performed after the patient has rested quietly for at least 5 minutes in a supine position. When the timing of ECG assessments coincides with a blood collection, the ECG should be obtained prior to the nominal time of the blood collection. In some cases, it may be appropriate to repeat an abnormal ECG to rule out improper lead placement as contributing to the ECG abnormality.

CBC results performed pre-dose on Days 1, 8, 15 and 22 of Cycle 1 must be available and reviewed by the investigator prior to trastuzumab, paclitaxel, or MLN0128 dosing.

Additionally, upon implementation of Amendment 1 and under Amendment 2, fasting blood glucose will be measured at 1 hour post-MLN0128 dosing on C1D2 when PK samples are drawn in the dose escalation phase only.

Microscopic urinalysis will also be performed along with a 12-hour urine collection and spot urine electrolytes, protein and creatinine, and serum chemistry at any time when the serum creatinine is ≥ Grade 1 according to NCI CTCAE version 4.0.

The starting dose of MLN0128 is 6 mg daily for the first 3 days (Days 1-3) of each week. Upon implementation of Amendment 1 in subsequent dose escalation cohorts, for the intermittent dosing schedule evaluated, MLN0128 is administered at approximately 24 hours following paclitaxel infusion during Weeks 1-3. For example, in the QDx3d QW dosing schedule, MLN0128 will be administered on Days 2-4, 9-11, 16-18, and 22-24 of each 4-week cycle; and, in the QDx5d QW dosing schedule, MLN0128 will be administered on Days 2-6, 9-13, 16-20, and 22-26 of each 4-week cycle. Electrolyte levels should be corrected as needed prior to MLN0128 dosing on days when paclitaxel is not administered. On days that paclitaxel is administered (with or without trastuzumab), electrolyte levels should be corrected prior to paclitaxel or trastuzumab dosing.

Paclitaxel 80 mg/m² will be given intravenously weekly on Days 1, 8 (±1 day), and 15 (±1 day) of every 28-day cycle according to the FDA label and institution’s standard clinical practice. Concomitant medications used prior to and during the administration of paclitaxel will be recorded on the appropriate CRF pages. Prior to Amendment 1, on days when paclitaxel was administered, MLN0128 was to be given orally in the clinic within 30 minutes of completion of the paclitaxel infusion; and upon implementation of Amendment 1, MLN0128 will be given orally at approximately 24 hours following paclitaxel infusion. If paclitaxel dosing is altered ±1 day, MLN0128 administration should also be altered accordingly. Electrolyte levels should be corrected as needed prior to paclitaxel dosing (see footnote 19 for days when trastuzumab is administered).

Once the MTD of MLN0128P (MTDMLN0128P) is determined for the dosing schedules evaluated, Arm B of the expansion phase will enroll HER2+ cancer subjects. These subjects will receive weekly trastuzumab in combination with MLN0128P at the MTDMLN0128P on Days 1, 8 (±1 day), 15 (±1 day) and 22 (±1 day) of every 28-day cycle. Trastuzumab 4 mg/kg loading dose will be given on C1D1, with subsequent weekly doses at 2 mg/kg thereafter. For subjects entering expansion Arm B already receiving trastuzumab, the C1D1 dose will be 2 mg/kg if the last trastuzumab dose had been administered within 28 days. If the last trastuzumab dose was administered >28 days before C1D1, then the 4 mg/kg loading dose will be administered. Trastuzumab will be administered intravenously prior to paclitaxel and according to the FDA label and institution’s standard clinical practice. On Day 22 (±1 day) when paclitaxel is not administered, MLN0128 will be administered within 30 minutes of the completion of the trastuzumab infusion. Concomitant medications used prior to and during the administration of trastuzumab will be recorded on the appropriate CRF pages. Electrolyte levels should be corrected as needed prior to trastuzumab dosing when trastuzumab is administered before paclitaxel.
Termination visit will be done within 30 days of last dose of MLN0128 or paclitaxel or trastuzumab, whichever is discontinued last.
Upon implementation of Amendment 2 subjects in the expansion phase were not required to return to clinic on C1D2.
The assessment does not need to be repeated if it was done within 3 days prior to the first dose.

12 lead ECG 4 hours (± 30 min) after MLN0128 dosing on Day 1 for subjects enrolled on the original protocol and 4 hours (± 30 min) post-dose on Day 2 for subjects enrolled following implementation of Amendment 1. Upon implementation of Amendment 2, ECG is only performed for subjects in the dose escalation phase on C1D2.
Tumor assessments will only be done on subjects who have not previously demonstrated disease progression in the study unless completed within the previous 4 weeks.
Height is only needed at Screening/Baseline.

Complete physical examination will be performed at Screening/Baseline, C1D1 and at the Termination visit. Symptom directed physical examination will be done on C1D8 (±1 day), C1D15 (±1 day) and C1D22 (±1 day).

All scheduled ECGs should be performed after the patient has rested quietly for at least 5 minutes in a supine position. When the timing of ECG assessments coincides with a blood collection, the ECG should be obtained prior to the nominal time of the blood collection. In some cases, it may be appropriate to repeat an abnormal ECG to rule out improper lead placement as contributing to the ECG abnormality.

Complete blood count (CBC) includes white blood cell count (WBC) with differential, hemoglobin (Hgb), hematocrit (Hct), and platelet count. CBC results performed pre-dose on Days 1, 8, 15 and 22 of Cycle 1 must be available and reviewed by the investigator prior to trastuzumab, paclitaxel, or MLN0128 dosing.

Full chemistry panel includes Chem 7 (sodium [Na], potassium [K] chloride [Cl], bicarbonate [CO₂], blood urea nitrogen [BUN], creatinine [Cr], glucose), liver function tests (LFTs - ALT, AST, alkaline phosphatase, total bilirubin, direct bilirubin), lactate dehydrogenase (LDH), total protein, calcium, phosphate, and magnesium. Chem 7 and LFT results performed pre-dose on Days 1, 8, 15, and 22 of Cycle 1 must be available and reviewed by the investigator prior to trastuzumab, paclitaxel, or MLN0128 dosing. Electrolyte levels should be corrected as needed before MLN0128 dosing, or before paclitaxel or trastuzumab dosing on days when paclitaxel or trastuzumab are administered 24 hours before MLN0128 dosing.

Fasting serum glucose will be measured prior to dexamethasone premedication for subjects receiving MLN0128P or prior to trastuzumab infusion for subjects receiving MLN0128PH on Cycle 1 Day 1 (C1D1), pre-MLN0128 dose on C1D22 when paclitaxel is not administered) for subjects receiving MLN0128PH on C1D8 (±1 day), C1D15 (±1 day), C1D22 (±1 day) and at the Termination visit. Additionally, upon implementation of Amendment 1 and under Amendment 2, fasting blood glucose will be measured at 1 hour post-MLN0128 dosing on C1D2 when PK samples are drawn in the dose escalation phase only.
Total cholesterol, high density lipoprotein cholesterol (HDL-C), low density lipoprotein cholesterol (LDL-C), and triglycerides.

Urinalysis will include macroscopic assessment of the amount of protein, glucose, white blood cells and blood if they are present (levels should be recorded if available) and microscopic analysis should be performed if an abnormality is noted. Microscopic urinalysis will also be performed along with a 12-hour urine collection and spot urine electrolytes, protein and creatinine, and serum chemistry at any time when the serum creatinine is ≥ Grade 1 according to NCI CTCAE version 4.0.
Only for female subjects with child-bearing potential. Serum pregnancy test will be done at Screening/Baseline. Either serum or urine pregnancy test will be done on C1D1.

Plasma PK samples will be obtained for subjects in the dose escalation phase prior to determination of the MLN0128P MTD at the following time points under the original protocol: pre-dose, immediately at the end of paclitaxel infusion (EOPI and pre-MLN0128 dose), and at 0.5 hour (±15 min), 1 hour (±15 min), 2 hours (±30 min), 4 hours (±30 min), 6 hours (± 30 min), and 24 ±2 hours post-MLN0128 dose on C1D1. The PK sample drawn on C1D22 (24 hours post-MLN0128 dosing) will be obtained before C1D2 MLN0128 dosing. Upon implementation of Amendment 1, plasma PK samples will be obtained for subjects in the dose escalation phase of the study at the following time points: on C1D1, pre-dose, immediately at EOPI, and at 0.5 hour (±15 min), 1 hour (±15 min), 2 hours (±30 min), 4 hours (±30 min), and 6 hours (±30 min) post-paclitaxel infusion completion; and on C1D2, pre-MLN0128 dose, and at 0.5 hour (±15 min), 1 hour (±15 min), 2 hours (±30 min), 4 hours (±30 min), and 6 hours (±30 min) post-MLN0128 C1D2 dose.

Fresh tumor tissue biopsies will be taken from subjects who have accessible tumor tissues based on the Investigator’s judgment and who have signed the consent for tumor biopsy. Fresh tumor tissue collections should coincide with the collection of PK samples at 3 hours ± 1 hour post-MLN0128 dose.

Every effort should be made for all subjects who have available archival tumor tissues to collect either paraffin blocks or a minimum of 10 unstained slides of archival tumor tissue for assessment of predictive/prognostic markers. Archival tumour tissues can be collected after the subject begins study drug treatment, if extra time is needed to locate the specimens.

Baseline CT (with contrast)/MRI of chest, abdomen and pelvis and relevant tumor markers (eg, cancer antigen [CA]125, prostate-specific antigen [PSA], CA19-9, CA27.29, CA15.3, and carcinoembryonic antigen [CEA]) must be obtained within 4 weeks prior to the first dose. A confirmatory scan should be performed at approximately 4 weeks from the previous scan for all subjects with an objective response of ≥ PR. The same method (CT with contrast or MRI) must be used throughout the study.

Subjects will be given a glucometer on C1D2 to monitor daily fasting whole blood glucose level at home and will be instructed to notify the study doctor when the fasting glucose is abnormal. The early detection and treatment of hyperglycemia has been key in managing this drug-related adverse event effectively in previous human studies with MLN0128. Each institution should decide an abnormal fasting glucose level (ie, ≥ 140 mg/dL) where the treatment and close monitoring of hyperglycemia might be required.
Adverse events will be collected after administration of the first dose of MLN0128, paclitaxel or trastuzumab through 30 days following the last dose of study drug.
Concomitant medications will be collected from 30 days prior to the first dose of study drug (MLN0128, paclitaxel or trastuzumab) until termination of the study.

Prior to Amendment 1, MLN0128 was initially to be given orally on a schedule consisting of daily administration of MLN0128 for 3 days on, 4 days off repeated each week (QDx3d QW of a 4-week cycle). On days when weekly paclitaxel is administered (Days 1, 8 [±1 day], and 15 [±1 day] of every 28-day cycle), MLN0128 was to be administered in the clinic to the subject within 30 minutes of the completion of paclitaxel infusion. On Day 22 (±1 day) when paclitaxel is not administered, MLN0128 was to be administered alone for subjects receiving MLN0128P and within 30 minutes of the completion of trastuzumab for subjects receiving MLN0128PH. The starting dose of MLN0128 is 6 mg daily for the first 3 days (Days 1-3) of each week. Upon implementation of Amendment 1 in subsequent dose escalation cohorts, for the intermittent dosing schedule evaluated, MLN0128 is administered at approximately 24 hours following paclitaxel infusion during Weeks 1-3. For example, in the QDx3d QW dosing schedule, MLN0128 will be administered on Days 2-4, 9-11, 16-18, and 22-24 of each 4-week cycle; and, in the QDx5d QW dosing schedule, MLN0128 will be administered on Days 2-6, 9-13, 16-20, and 22-26 of each 4-week cycle. Electrolyte levels should be corrected as needed prior to MLN0128 dosing on days when paclitaxel is not administered. On days that paclitaxel is administered (with or without trastuzumab), electrolyte levels should be corrected prior to paclitaxel or trastuzumab dosing.

Concomitant medications used prior to and during the administration of paclitaxel will be recorded on the appropriate CRF pages. Prior to Amendment 1, on days when paclitaxel was administered, MLN0128 was to be given orally in the clinic within 30 minutes of completion of the paclitaxel infusion; and upon implementation of Amendment 1, MLN0128 will be given orally at approximately 24 hours following paclitaxel infusion. If paclitaxel dosing is altered ±1 day, MLN0128 administration should also be altered accordingly. Electrolyte levels should be corrected as needed prior to paclitaxel dosing (see footnote 19 for days when trastuzumab is administered).

Once the MTD of MLN0128P (MTDMLN0128P) is determined for the dosing schedules evaluated, Arm B of the expansion phase will enroll HER2+ cancer subjects. These subjects will receive weekly trastuzumab in combination with MLN0128P at the MTDMLN0128P on Days 1, 8 (±1 day), 15 (±1 day) and 22 (±1 day) of every 28-day cycle. Trastuzumab 4 mg/kg loading dose will be given on C1D1, with subsequent weekly doses at 2 mg/kg thereafter. For subjects entering expansion Arm B already receiving trastuzumab, the C1D1 dose will be 2 mg/kg if the last trastuzumab dose had been administered within 28 days. If the last trastuzumab dose was administered >28 days before C1D1, then the 4 mg/kg loading dose will be administered. Trastuzumab will be administered intravenously prior to paclitaxel and according to the FDA label and institution’s standard clinical practice. On Day 22 (±1 day) when paclitaxel is not administered, MLN0128 will be administered within 30 minutes of the completion of the trastuzumab infusion. Concomitant medications used prior to and during the administration of trastuzumab will be recorded on the appropriate CRF pages. Electrolyte levels should be corrected as needed prior to trastuzumab dosing when trastuzumab is administered before paclitaxel.
Termination visit will be done within 30 days of last dose of MLN0128 or paclitaxel or trastuzumab, whichever is discontinued last.
Upon implementation of Amendment 2 subjects in the expansion phase were not required to return to clinic on C1D2.
The assessment does not need to be repeated if it was done within 3 days prior to the first dose.

12 lead ECG 4 hours (± 30 min) after MLN0128 dosing on Day 1 for subjects enrolled on the original protocol and 4 hours (± 30 min) post-dose on Day 2 for subjects enrolled following implementation of Amendment 1. Upon implementation of Amendment 2, ECG is only performed for subjects in the dose escalation phase on C1D2.
Tumor assessments will only be done on subjects who have not previously demonstrated disease progression in the study unless completed within the previous 4 weeks.
Height is only needed at Screening/Baseline.

In some cases, it may be appropriate to repeat an abnormal ECG to rule out improper lead placement as contributing to the ECG abnormality.

Additionally, upon implementation of Amendment 1 and under Amendment 2, fasting blood glucose will be measured at 1 hour post-MLN0128 dosing on C1D2 when PK samples are drawn in the dose escalation phase only.
Total cholesterol, high density lipoprotein cholesterol (HDL-C), low density lipoprotein cholesterol (LDL-C), and triglycerides.

Microscopic urinalysis will also be performed along with a 12-hour urine collection and spot urine electrolytes, protein and creatinine, and serum chemistry at any time when the serum creatinine is ≥ Grade 1 according to NCI CTCAE version 4.0.

Concomitant medications will be collected from 30 days prior to the first dose of study drug (MLN0128, paclitaxel or trastuzumab) until termination of the study.

12 lead ECG 4 hours (± 30 min) after MLN0128 dosing on Day 1 for subjects enrolled on the original protocol and 4 hours (± 30 min) post-dose on Day 2 for subjects enrolled following implementation of Amendment 1. Upon implementation of Amendment 2, ECG is only performed for subjects in the dose escalation phase on C1D2.
Tumor assessments will only be done on subjects who have not previously demonstrated disease progression in the study unless completed within the previous 4 weeks.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Age ≥ 18 years, including males and females;
eligibilityCriteria.text: Age ≥ 18 years, including males and females;

eligibilityCriteria.name: For women of child-bearing potential, negative serum pregnancy test...
eligibilityCriteria.text: For women of child-bearing potential, negative serum pregnancy test within 14 days prior to the first study drug administration and use of physician-approved method of birth control from 30 days prior to the first study drug administration to 90 days following the last study drug administration;

eligibilityCriteria.name: Known human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) infection;
eligibilityCriteria.text: Known human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) infection;

eligibilityCriteria.name: Subjects must have locally advanced or metastatic solid tumors with the except...
eligibilityCriteria.text: Subjects must have locally advanced or metastatic solid tumors with the exception of primary brain tumor, and have failed or are not eligible for standard of care therapy. Subjects with a history of brain metastasis are eligible for the study as long as they meet all the following criteria: their brain metastases have been treated, they have no evidence of progression or hemorrhage after treatment, have been off dexamethasone for 4 weeks prior to first study drug administration, and have no ongoing requirement for dexamethasone or anti-epileptic drugs;

eligibilityCriteria.name: For Arm A of the expansion phase only, subjects with advanced bladder...
eligibilityCriteria.text: For Arm A of the expansion phase only, subjects with advanced bladder cancer or endometrial cancer will be enrolled and must have received at least 1, but no more than 2 prior lines of systemic therapy, including adjuvant or hormonal therapy;

eligibilityCriteria.name: For Arm B of the expansion phase only, HER2+ cancer is defined as a path...
eligibilityCriteria.text: For Arm B of the expansion phase only, HER2+ cancer is defined as a pathologic diagnosis of cancer which is 3+ by immunohistochemistry (IHC) for HER2 or positive for HER2 gene amplification by fluorescence in situ hybridization (FISH) and subjects must have received at least 1, but no more than 4 prior lines of systemic cytotoxic chemotherapy, excluding adjuvant, hormonal, or targeted therapy;

eligibilityCriteria.name: Subjects must have received no more than 4 prior lines of systemic cy...
eligibilityCriteria.text: Subjects must have received no more than 4 prior lines of systemic cytotoxic chemotherapy for advanced or metastatic disease;

eligibilityCriteria.name: Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group (ECOG) performance status (PS) 0-1;
eligibilityCriteria.text: Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group (ECOG) performance status (PS) 0-1;

eligibilityCriteria.name: Subjects must have adequate organ function, including the following: a. B...
eligibilityCriteria.text: Subjects must have adequate organ function, including the following: a. Bone marrow reserve consistent with: absolute neutrophil count (ANC) ≥ 1.5 x10 9 /L; platelet count ≥ 100 x 10 9 /L; hemoglobin ≥ 9 g/dL without transfusion in the last 2 weeks; b. Hepatic: total bilirubin ≤ 1.5 x upper ULN, transaminases (aspartate aminotransferase/serum glutamic oxaloacetic transaminase-AST/SGOT and alanine aminotransferase/serum glutamic pyruvic transaminase-ALT/SGPT) ≤ 2.5 x ULN (< 5 x ULN if liver metastases are present); c. Renal: normal serum creatinine or calculated creatinine clearance ≥ 60 mL/min (Cockroft - Gault formula); d. Metabolic: fasting serum glucose ≤ 130 mg/dL and fasting triglycerides ≤ 300 mg/dL;

eligibilityCriteria.name: Left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) ≥ 5 absolute percentage points...
eligibilityCriteria.text: Left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) ≥ 5 absolute percentage points below institutional standard of normal as measured by echocardiogram (ECHO) or multiple gated acquisition scan (MUGA) within 4 weeks prior to first study drug administration (ie, if the institutional normal is 50%, subject's LVEF may be as low as 45% to be eligible for the study);

eligibilityCriteria.name: Male subjects must be surgically sterile or must agree to use physician...
eligibilityCriteria.text: Male subjects must be surgically sterile or must agree to use physician-approved contraception during the study and for 90 days following the last study drug administration;

eligibilityCriteria.name: Ability to swallow oral medications;
eligibilityCriteria.text: Ability to swallow oral medications;

eligibilityCriteria.name: Ability to understand and willingness to sign informed consent form p...
eligibilityCriteria.text: Ability to understand and willingness to sign informed consent form prior to initiation of any study procedures;

eligibilityCriteria.name: Diagnosis of primary brain tumor;
eligibilityCriteria.text: Diagnosis of primary brain tumor;

eligibilityCriteria.name: Have received prior cancer or other investigational therapy within 2 w...
eligibilityCriteria.text: Have received prior cancer or other investigational therapy within 2 weeks prior to the first administration of study drug. For prior therapies with a half life longer than 3 days, the interval must be at least 28 days prior to the first administration of study drug and the subject must have documented disease progression. For prior trastuzumab therapy in subjects who enroll in Arm B of the expansion phase, the interval since the previous trastuzumab dose must be at least 7 days;

eligibilityCriteria.name: Have initiated hematopoietic growth factors within 1 week prior to the...
eligibilityCriteria.text: Have initiated hematopoietic growth factors within 1 week prior to the first administration of study drug; subjects already receiving hematopoietic growth factors on a chronic basis for ≥ 4 weeks are eligible;

eligibilityCriteria.name: Chronic systemic corticosteroid (except inhalers) use within 1 week p...
eligibilityCriteria.text: Chronic systemic corticosteroid (except inhalers) use within 1 week prior to the first administration of study drug. Premedication with dexamethasone prior to paclitaxel administration in this study is allowed;

eligibilityCriteria.name: Manifestations of malabsorption due to prior gastrointestinal (GI) sur...
eligibilityCriteria.text: Manifestations of malabsorption due to prior gastrointestinal (GI) surgery, GI disease, or for an unknown reason that may alter the absorption of MLN0128;

eligibilityCriteria.name: Poorly controlled diabetes mellitus defined as HbA1c > 7%; subjects ...
eligibilityCriteria.text: Poorly controlled diabetes mellitus defined as HbA1c > 7%; subjects with transient glucose intolerance due to corticosteroid administration are allowed in this study if all other inclusion/exclusion criteria are met;

eligibilityCriteria.name: Other clinically significant co-morbidities, such as uncontrolled pulmon...
eligibilityCriteria.text: Other clinically significant co-morbidities, such as uncontrolled pulmonary disease, active central nervous system (CNS) disease, active infection, or any other condition that could compromise the subject's participation in the study;

eligibilityCriteria.name: Pregnancy (positive serum or urine pregnancy test) or breast feeding;
eligibilityCriteria.text: Pregnancy (positive serum or urine pregnancy test) or breast feeding;

eligibilityCriteria.name: Any history of unstable angina, myocardial infarction, New York Heart A...
eligibilityCriteria.text: Any history of unstable angina, myocardial infarction, New York Heart Association (NYHA) Class III or IV heart failure (See Appendix C), and/or pulmonary hypertension;

eligibilityCriteria.name: Significant active cardiovascular disease including: o Uncontrolled high...
eligibilityCriteria.text: Significant active cardiovascular disease including: o Uncontrolled high blood pressure (ie, systolic blood pressure >180 mm Hg, diastolic blood pressure > 95 mm Hg) o Grade 3 or higher valvular disease o Grade 3 or higher atrial fibrillation o Grade 3 or higher bradycardia o Endocarditis o Pulmonary embolism o Recent cerebrovascular accident within 6 months prior to enrollment

eligibilityCriteria.name: A requirement for inotropic support or serious uncontrolled cardiac ar...
eligibilityCriteria.text: A requirement for inotropic support or serious uncontrolled cardiac arrhythmia (including atrial flutter/fibrillation) within 1 year prior to screening

eligibilityCriteria.name: A pacemaker or implantable cardiac defibrillator;
eligibilityCriteria.text: A pacemaker or implantable cardiac defibrillator;

eligibilityCriteria.name: Baseline prolongation of the rate-corrected QT interval (QTc) (eg, repe...
eligibilityCriteria.text: Baseline prolongation of the rate-corrected QT interval (QTc) (eg, repeated demonstration of QTc interval > 480 milliseconds) (See Section 7.8.2.2);

eligibilityCriteria.name: History of congenital long QT syndrome, ventricular fibrillation, ventr...
eligibilityCriteria.text: History of congenital long QT syndrome, ventricular fibrillation, ventricular tachycardia or torsades de pointes.

studyInterventions.name: MLN0128

role.decode: Investigational Product - the novel drug or treatment being evaluated in the study

type.decode: Small molecule drug or chemical compound

studyInterventions.label: MLN0128
studyInterventions.description: Investigational product administered orally in combination with paclitaxel on QDx3d QW or alternate schedules such as QDx5d QW in 4-week cycles.

administrations.name: MLN0128 6 mg QDx3d QW

duration.text: 4-week cycles until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity
duration.reasonDurationWillVary: Treatment continues in the absence of disease progression or unacceptable treatment-related toxicity

administrations.description: Administered orally once daily with a meal on Days 2-4, 9-11, 16-18, 22-24 of each 28-day cycle, approximately 24 hours following paclitaxel infusion where applicable. Dose may be escalated per protocol or reduced to 4 mg based on toxicity.

administrations.name: MLN0128 6 mg QDx5d QW

duration.text: 4-week cycles until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity
duration.reasonDurationWillVary: Treatment continues in the absence of disease progression or unacceptable treatment-related toxicity

administrations.description: Administered orally once daily with a meal on Days 2-6, 9-13, 16-20, 22-26 of each 28-day cycle, approximately 24 hours following paclitaxel infusion where applicable. Dose may be escalated per protocol or reduced to 4 mg based on toxicity.

studyInterventions.name: Paclitaxel

type.decode: Small molecule drug or chemical compound

studyInterventions.label: Paclitaxel
studyInterventions.description: Background chemotherapy administered weekly in combination with MLN0128.

administrations.name: Paclitaxel 80 mg/m²

duration.text: 4-week cycles until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity
duration.reasonDurationWillVary: Treatment continues in the absence of disease progression or unacceptable treatment-related toxicity

administrations.description: Administered as a 1-hour IV infusion on Days 1, 8 (±1 day), and 15 (±1 day) of each 28-day cycle. Premedication per institutional standard. Dose may be reduced by 25% based on toxicity.

studyInterventions.name: Trastuzumab

type.decode: Biologic, antibody, protein, cell therapy, or gene therapy product

studyInterventions.label: Trastuzumab
studyInterventions.description: Additional required medicinal product for HER2+ subjects in expansion Arm B, administered weekly in combination with MLN0128P.

administrations.name: Trastuzumab loading dose
administrations.description: Loading dose administered IV on Cycle 1 Day 1 prior to paclitaxel.

administrations.name: Trastuzumab maintenance dose

duration.text: 4-week cycles until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity
duration.reasonDurationWillVary: Treatment continues in the absence of disease progression or unacceptable treatment-related toxicity

administrations.description: Maintenance dose administered IV weekly on Days 1, 8 (±1 day), 15 (±1 day), 22 (±1 day) of each 28-day cycle, prior to paclitaxel where applicable. For subjects previously on trastuzumab, 2 mg/kg if last dose within 28 days; otherwise loading dose.
